resolvedQuestions: Q1: Is folding deep-loop-runtime INTO deep-loop-workflows (as system-deep-loop) mechanically safe, and where does each component land?
Q2: How many bidirectional path-couplings exist between the two skills, and what breaks silently if a coupling is not repaired?
Q3: How does the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow (node_modules reach-ins + artifact-root re-export) behave post-merge, and is merging a net simplification or a net risk transfer?
Q4: What is the full reference-migration surface across commands/agents/READMEs/advisor-corpus/test literals/named profile files, and what is the risk of incomplete migration?
Q5: Should fallback-router.ts be wired for real GLM-5.2 -> MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback given it has zero callers today?

keyFindings.summary: Detached fan-out lineage boundary conflicts with YAML spec.md mutation (not gated on fanout_lineage_artifact_dir).
Convergence-floor test (REQ-002 enforcer) hardcodes old skill name; Class A test-file count understated (7->10).
Class B reverse-coupling inventory incomplete: reduce-state/runtime-capabilities shims, orchestrate-session.cjs, continuity-thread require.
NEW third path-repair class: replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs repo-root absolute existence-probe breaks silently.
Council test fixtures are semantic data, not path references; add to residual-grep allowlist.
test:council include glob can silently shrink the test set; needs non-empty-match assertion.
Divergence-ledger reason prose embeds old name; must update with structured fields.
Named benchmark profile deep-loop-runtime.json has ambiguous rename target.
classifyLineageFailure routes quota/auth to FATAL, making fallback wiring unreachable for GLM quota-out.
Registry id vs provider slug mismatch needs a normalizer before resolveFallback.
External-prose surface broad: 10 agent files + README + 5 sibling graphs needing edge-collapse.

ruledOutDirections: runtime/ as a mode-registry entry (category error)
Per-replica spec.md writeback in fan-out (races on generated fence)
replay-graph as a Class B relative-hop site (repo-root absolute probe)
Global find/replace of deep-loop-runtime in council tests (semantic data)
Decoupling runtime TS tooling during merge (failure modes indistinguishable)
Dynamic mode-registry reads on advisor hot path (intentional coupling avoidance)
Naive single-call-site fallback wiring (unreachable for quota/auth)
Renaming /deep:* commands or public agent names (public surfaces stable)
